metaTitle: Getting Started — Prosperity | Loot Overhaul for Minecraft
metaDescription: A linear walkthrough — install Prosperity, learn how instanced loot works, read the unlooted indicators, and understand the five distance tiers.
ogDescription: Install Prosperity, learn how instanced loot works, and understand distance tiers.
title: Getting Started
lede: A linear walkthrough — install Prosperity, understand how instanced loot and indicators work, and learn to read the five distance tiers.

html: Prosperity is a Fabric mod for Minecraft 1.21.1 with zero external dependencies beyond Fabric API. You'll need:

items: Install Fabric Loader 0.16.10+ via the official Fabric installer, targeting Minecraft 1.21.1. Prosperity requires Java 21.
Download Fabric API and drop it into your mods/ folder.
Download Prosperity from the releases page and drop the jar into mods/ — on both the server and every client.
Launch the game once. The first run generates .minecraft/config/prosperity.json with default values.

html: Migrating from Lootr? Remove it first. Prosperity provides its own instancing layer and the two should not run together.
See the full config reference for every available setting.

The first time you open a naturally generated container, Prosperity rolls loot just for you and stores it under your UUID. Other players who open the same chest get their own independent loot — no more racing to the good chests on a shared server.

The vanilla block is never replaced; instanced loot is attached to the container behind the scenes. Hoppers and comparators see an empty vanilla inventory, which is intended — your loot lives in your instance.
A gold sparkle hovers above any container you haven't opened yet. Once you loot it, the sparkle disappears for you. In large structures this tells you at a glance what's left to explore.
Loot quality scales with how far a container is from world origin. The five tiers run Local → Frontier → Wilderness → Outlands → Depths, each raising stack quantities and quality:
Run /prosperity info to see your current tier, distance, and multipliers. Some structures override their tier — see structure overrides.

Prefer a glance to a chat command? Bind Peek Loot Detail under Controls → Prosperity (unbound by default) and hold it to overlay your distance, current tier and progress, the full tier ladder, and the unlooted containers around you right now — see Peek Loot Detail.
